date_notes: Court of King's Bench granted Elections Alberta a temporary injunction April 30 2026. Permanent-injunction hearing set for late July 2026. Cease-and-desist letters issued May 6-7 2026.
title_en: Court orders separatist group to remove searchable database of 2.9 million voters' data
title_fr: Un tribunal ordonne à un groupe séparatiste de retirer une base de données consultable de 2,9 millions d'électeurs
description_en: A Court of King's Bench judge grants Elections Alberta a temporary injunction ordering the Centurion Project — a pro-sovereignty group registered as a third-party advertiser — to take down a publicly searchable database containing the names, home addresses, phone numbers and elector IDs of approximately 2.9 million Albertans. Elections Alberta states the data originated from the List of Electors legitimately issued to the Republican Party of Alberta and was then used by the Centurion Project in a manner the agency says was unauthorized; third-party advertisers are not eligible to receive the List of Electors. The RCMP opens a parallel investigation and Alberta's Information and Privacy Commissioner launches an investigation under PIPA. Elections Alberta issues cease-and-desist letters to 568 people (23 who received the list and 545 who accessed it). A permanent-injunction hearing is set for late July 2026.

actors.display_role: Court of King's Bench of Alberta
